title: Scars
subtitle: Treat it, carry it, weaponize it

content.text: The scar is the receipt. Death came to collect, and the body paid in the only currency it prints.
Scars are where death goes instead of happening. Every drop you survive leaves one — The Staircase covers how. This page covers what the debt costs, and the three things you can do with it.
Scar remediation is classified as elective care. Untreated scarring does not affect your ability to work. It affects only your ability to continue doing so. Clinics are standing by.
A scar is a debt, and you hold it the way you hold any debt: pay it off, make the minimum payments, or borrow against it.

Treat it. Credits and downtime close a scar for good. Treatment is its own line on the budget — it is not training, and every credit and downtime period it eats is one that training does not get. The long bill runs through a Street Doc and the Medicine skill (see Street Doc).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Scar treatment prices — credits and downtime per scar, and how provider quality moves them — are not yet specified.
Carry it. An untreated scar rides along and worsens your odds: every one you carry raises the DC of your next Fate Roll. The arithmetic lives on The Staircase.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Beyond the worse Fate odds, the specific debuffs a carried scar imposes are not yet specified.
Weaponize it. One tree in the game wants your scars: Fury converts them into accuracy and damage on a climbing ladder. The ladder and every number on it live on Fury.
Your sheet keeps two counts: scars carried untreated, and scars paid off. Only the untreated count works against you.
Every untreated scar raises your Fate Roll DC — formula on The Staircase.
At zero untreated scars, a drop converts to an automatic scar with no roll (The Staircase). Treating your way back to zero buys back that clean fall.
A treated scar is closed business: it no longer counts toward your Fate DC.

content.label: THE SCAR LAW
content.text: Scar-fed power may ease living with scars — never dying with them. Nothing built on your scars can modify the Fate Roll, lower its DC, or change how scars are counted. No augment, no mutation, no exception.
Fury makes scars worth carrying. It does not make them safer to carry. If a power seems to promise better odds on the Fate Roll, read it again — it does not, anywhere, ever.
Treatment and training drink from the same account. So does everything else you want. Decide what the scar is worth before the next drop decides for you.
